analyst_observations: The skill achieves 100% pass rate across all assertions and all test cases, vs 43-57% for the baseline.

The three assertions that consistently fail without the skill are: mandatory living-document sections (Progress, Surprises, Decision Log, Outcomes), checkbox-based progress tracking, and plain-language jargon definitions. These are the skill's primary value-add.

The skill costs ~55% more tokens and ~44% more time on average. This is expected since the with-skill outputs are significantly longer and more structured (371 lines for small PRD vs 265 without skill).
Both configurations perform well on: concrete file paths, runnable commands, observable outcomes, and self-containment. These appear to be baseline capabilities rather than skill-driven improvements.

For the ambiguous PRD, both configurations flag the ambiguities — but only the skill version documents resolutions in a formal Decision Log. The baseline uses an assumptions table that is not a living-document section.

The skill's strongest differentiator is producing plans that are 'restartable' — a stateless agent can pick up the plan mid-implementation because Progress, Decision Log, and Surprises sections exist for tracking state.
